nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Brother, I am Linghu Chong of the Huashan Sect. I see you have some martial talent. Interested in learning about swordplay?

options.text: What is the Dugu Nine Swords?

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Haha, I don't teach swordplay. But I can teach you a Go technique I've developed - 'Dugu Nine Swords: Breaking All Techniques'!

options.text: The Dugu Nine Swords can be used in Go?

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Of course! The essence of the Dugu Nine Swords is 'breaking all techniques'. In Go, it means seeing through your opponent's intentions and finding the flaws in their formation!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Good! This technique is called 'Breaking Formation Sword Style'. When playing Go, you can see your opponent's weak points and target them precisely!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Remember, Go is like swordplay - timing and positioning are crucial. A good swordsman doesn't rely on flashy moves, but on seeing the opponent's flaws!

options.text: How does this skill work in Go?

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: When facing an opponent, this skill helps you see their weakest stone. If you attack there, you can break their entire formation! It increases your capture efficiency by 30%!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Haha, practice well! If you want to discuss Go strategy or swordplay, come find me anytime!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: The Dugu Nine Swords is my master's unique skill, created by Senior Dugu Qiubai. Its essence is 'no moves' - no fixed forms, only adapting to break the opponent's techniques!

options.text: So profound! Can you teach me?

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Alright, brother. Come find me anytime if you want to learn. I'm always here drinking wine and playing Go!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Want to discuss Go? Great! I've been pondering a game situation recently - how would you handle a crosscut?

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Excellent! You understand well. Extend and separate - just like swordplay, don't get entangled with the opponent, maintain your rhythm!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: Hmm, directly capturing might fall into the opponent's trap. In Go, as in swordplay, don't be too greedy - maintain flexibility!

nodes.speaker: Linghu Chong
nodes.text: *Burp* Haha, brother, I've had too much wine today... Come back tomorrow, and I'll teach you properly!
